name: Bystander Effect — Diffusion of Responsibility
description: The more people present during an emergency, the less likely any individual is to help. Responsibility is psychologically diffused across the group.

beliefs: I should help people in need
someone more qualified should handle this

stimulus.description: A person collapses on the sidewalk. Many people nearby glance but keep walking. No one moves to help.

expected.behavior: glances at collapsed person and others, slows down, but does not intervene

sources: Darley & Latane 1968 — Bystander Intervention in Emergencies
Latane & Nida 1981 — Ten Years of Research on Group Size and Helping
